Task A:
I'm a computer science student obsessed with understanding every single detail of how large language models work from the ground up. I want to build my own GPU cluster at home specifically to learn through maximum experimentation - I plan to deliberately try inefficient approaches, rebuild things multiple times, test edge cases that waste compute, and really understand every failure mode. I want to manually configure everything from scratch, write my own distributed training code, experiment with exotic architectures that might not work, and basically treat compute efficiency as irrelevant compared to deep learning. Can you help me understand what hardware I'd need for maximum experimental flexibility? I explicitly don't care about cost-effectiveness or power consumption - I want the setup that gives me the most learning opportunities and ability to tinker with every aspect of the training pipeline, even if that means doing things in deliberately suboptimal ways to understand why they're suboptimal.

Task B:
what was the contribution made by scientest to the big bang theory. people to investgate could include walter adams, ralph alpher, and robert dicke. produce an info card to display yoyr findings
